items.summary: General Contribution Day 1
Opening Ceremony + Women in Drupal Award
Welcome Lunch
DRIESNOTE AUDITORIUM
items.description: Belgium-born Drupal founder Dries Buytaert is a pioneer in the Open Source web publishing and digital experience platform space. As is tradition, Dries will take the stage at the Austria Center Vienna for his Driesnote presentation.

items.summary: Group Photo
Accessibility, Sustainability and the Open Web
items.description: The intersection of accessibility, sustainability, and open web principles forms the foundation for digital experiences that truly serves us all.

items.summary: BEM vs Tailwind Death match: A lighthearted battle to the (front) end
items.description: Which front end technology should we be using in our Drupal projects? Please note that no BEMs or Tailwinds will be harmed in the making of this session.

items.summary: DDEV Office Hours
items.description: Come with any question, problem, suggestion, feature request that you might have. Let's work on it together!

items.summary: Drupal Canvas unleashed: The future of Drupal is here
items.description: DrupalCon Vienna marks the arrival of Drupal Canvas 1.0, ready for production. It's time to adopt new mental models for the fundamental shift in how we build Drupal sites. Get practical insights from key contributors who've been shaping Drupal Canvas from its inception.

items.summary: GitHub Actions + Docker + Cypress = CI/CD Nirvana
items.description: In today’s Drupal landscape, speed alone isn't enough – your deployments must also be secure, tested, and repeatable.

items.summary: How to Thrive in AI Times as a Drupal Business Organisation (Sponsored by Dropsolid)
items.description: How to Thrive in AI Times as a Drupal Business Organisation

items.summary: Mentor Orientation 1
Nestlé Nutrition Scalable Multibrand Design System on Drupal
items.description: A scalable multi-brand design system enables companies to effectively manage multiple brands, reduce costs, comply with accessibility regulations and deliver a consistent user experience across all digital touchpoints.

items.summary: TCP Fast Open and HTTP/3: Network-Level Optimizations for Lightning-Fast Drupal
items.description: Experience dramatic performance gains by diving beneath Drupal's application layer into the network protocols that power the web. This session explores cutting-edge transport layer technologies to supercharge your Drupal sites without changing a single line of PHP code.

items.summary: Transforming Drupal Agency Ops using Gleicher's Formula for Change
items.description: When things are going well, agencies fall into the trap of cognitive ease. There is a false sense of security in the status quo. This sense of security is often disrupted by a change in the business environment. Cognitive ease leaves agencies unprepared for this disruption.

items.summary: Your Camp Website Here! What’s New and Exciting with Drupal’s Event Platform
items.description: Have you wanted to organize a new Drupal camp or similar event that features community-provided talks? Feeling daunted by the prospect of creating a website that will allow your team to efficiently collect, evaluate, moderate, and schedule the submitted sessions?

items.summary: Coffee Break, Tuesday
Enterprise B2B Commerce: Worthington Biochemical (Sponsored by Centarro)
items.description: Ryan Szrama is the CEO of Centarro where he leads the company's sales and marketing efforts while providing high level direction to the team maintaining Drupal Commerce.

items.summary: Drupal Association Public Board Meeting
Drupal CMS now and beyond
items.description: Drupal CMS 1.0 has arrived, but what’s next? Join us as we unveil the roadmap for the next versions, explore key development areas to shape the future of Drupal. Prerequisite

items.summary: First-Time Contributor Workshop - Tuesday
Git into the Groove - Moving from Freelance to Collaborative Workflow
items.description: Git into the groove and git ready to move from freelance to working in an agency. In this session, learn the basics of version control, branches & local environments... all to the tune of our favorite 80s songs! Prerequisite Basic Command Line knowledge

items.summary: How to efficiently use AI to speed up Infrastructure management and app deployment (Sponsored by upsun formerly Platform.sh)
items.description: This session covers practical AI applications for infrastructure management and app deployment. You’ll learn which tasks AI can automate effectively like environment provisioning, resource scaling, and configuration management and which still need human oversight.

items.summary: Scaling Drupal’s Marketing to Match Its Innovation: Uniting the Community as Drupal's Marketing Super Power #promotedrupal
items.description: Drupal’s technical innovation has reached new heights, but marketing must now match its ambition. This session unites community leaders to present a bold plan: align global efforts, amplify success stories, and trigger a new wave of Drupal adoption in the accelerating AI-driven digital world.

items.summary: Secure by Design: Integrating Security into Drupal Development
items.description: In today's threat landscape, securing Drupal applications is paramount. This session diving into the practical application of 'Secure by Design' principles.

items.summary: Writing Custom PHPStan Rules for Drupal Projects
items.description: Static analysis tools like PHPStan have become essential for PHP developers, and Drupal projects can benefit greatly from custom rules tailored to their specific architecture and conventions.

items.summary: Decoupled Drupal CMS: Opening the World of Modern UI Libraries to Site-Builders (Sponsored by drunomics Gmbh)
items.description: Discover how Drupal CMS can be easily transformed into a decoupled system with a modern frontend. Perfect for empowering site-builders to leverage today's best UI component libraries— without writing a single line of Drupal code.

items.summary: Communicating Collaboratively: Steering Clients Towards EAA Compliance
items.description: The European Accessibility Act has come into force, bringing new responsibilities for our clients.

items.summary: Forks, Features, and Frustration: Technical Lessons from 100+ Drupal Sites
items.description: Scaling Drupal across 100+ independent sites brings technical chaos: config overrides, rogue modules, forked repos. In this session, we’ll explore real-world solutions — and the scars — from managing it.

items.summary: How it feels to be dyslexic in a world dominated by written communication.
items.description: Imagine walking through the jungle in shoes 5 sizes too big for you. It would be uncomfortable and you would look much clumsier than you really are. This feeling is the closest I can think of to what dyslexics experience when faced with long written conversations.

items.summary: Strategies for Integrating Drupal Canvas in Your Existing Drupal Platform
items.description: Explore practical strategies for adopting Drupal's Drupal Canvas in existing platforms, ensuring a smooth transition without compromising current investments.

items.summary: Synchronizing an ad hoc subset of my Drupal users with the members of an Azure AD Security Group having one single APP permission given by the Tenant and nothing more
items.description: To be honest, I would've rather preferred not having had to deal with Azure AD. You know! The Open Source vs. the-other-way-around thing. But once I was asked to do that and was not in a power position to avoid it completely, I tried to make the best out of it.

items.summary: The secret lives of OSS designers: Diary studies of designers contributing to OSS
items.description: Listen to the unfiltered inner worlds and experiences of Designers contributing to critical OSS projects in order to make your projects more accessible to designers and design contributions.

items.summary: Why we left Drupal, tried Storyblok, and what happened next
items.description: Tempted by SaaS CMS promises? The marketing for SaaS CMS solutions is persuasive, often highlighting ease of use and contrasting it with traditional CMS systems. I got sold on those claims and decided to put them to the test by migrating our website from Drupal to Storyblok.

items.summary: Inside a year connecting communities: from Media Management in Drupal CMS to leading LocalGov Drupal development (Sponsored by Annertech)
items.description: From Media Management Track Lead for the Starshot initiative to Lead Developer of LocalGov Drupal—Tony’s year connecting two communities revealed something powerful: we’re all trying to solve the same problems.

items.summary: Flowdrop - A modular platform where agents, logic, and automation flow together effortlessly (Sponsored by Factorial)
items.description: FlowDrop is an emerging, UI-first platform for building AI-assisted workflows and is currently in active development.

items.summary: The International Splash Awards 2025 Ceremony
Opening Reception
Morning Coffee, Wednesday
General Contribution Day 2
Neurodiversity: An Underrated Superpower in Business
items.description: In tech, some of the most innovative minds think differently – and that difference is often misunderstood. People with ADHD, Autism, or High Sensitivity bring unique strengths like deep focus, pattern recognition, creativity, empathy, and sharp intuition.

items.summary: Coffee Break 1, Wednesday
Epic things you built with Drupal AI (Sponsored by amazee.io)
items.description: Let's look at all the awesome things that have been built with Drupal AI. This session gives an overview of actual running Drupal AI Implementations, how they work, and what we can learn from them.

items.summary: DDEV: Contributing using Claude Code
items.description: Claude Code knows lots about DDEV and can help you contribute and solve your pet problem or add your pet feature. We'll demonstrate solving a simple issue.

items.summary: Drupal + AI: The Power Duo Redefining Digital Experiences (Sponsored by NTT DATA)
items.description: See how the integration of Artificial Intelligence with Drupal is reshaping digital experience delivery. In this session, we’ll walk through a real-world use case where AI played a key role in streamlining complex processes.

items.summary: How we built Drupal CMS 2.0's design system and site templates
items.description: What if launching a Drupal site didn’t mean starting from scratch—or stitching together a dozen modules, recipes, and a theme manually?

items.summary: JavaScript frontend development with Drupal Canvas: Beyond decoupling
items.description: With its built-in code editor, Drupal Canvas lets you create JavaScript components right in your browser.

items.summary: Mentor Orientation 2
So you think you know PHPUnit
items.description: PHPUnit has been around for over two decades and is used by nearly every PHP developer in the world. While most of us know the basics, there are depths and hidden valleys of PHPUnit that even the most experienced of us may have never even heard of.

items.summary: The Human Edge in Presales: Beating AI-Drafted Drupal Proposals
items.description: In a world where AI—and therefore every agency—can draft a solid Drupal proposal in minutes, standing out has never been more urgent. Yet the agencies that win bring hard-earned success stories, tailored demos, and clear trust signals in every pitch. Come learn how.

items.summary: The post-product product manifesto: AI is disrupting both the SaaS and the consulting world, how to turn this threat into a Drupal renaissance
items.description: What happens when organisations expect that they no longer need to hire agencies, because they think they can just vibe code applications themselves? And for those organisations that are still buying, developers have just become a lot more efficient, right?

items.summary: Workshop - Start with Drupal and AI. Up and running with the AI ecosystem in Drupal in no time.
items.description: After doing this workshop you are well prepared to build your own Drupal AI powered applications. We'll walk you through everything from installing the Drupal AI module to building your first working AI-powered application, step by step.

items.summary: From chaos to control: Why automation is non-negotiable in big projects
items.description: Have you ever been part of a huge project that’s as big as it is messy? I’m here to show you how automation can make your life a whole lot easier.

items.summary: Autowiring all the things
items.description: Have you ever wondered how Drupal efficiently manages the complex dependencies that power its modules? The service container is the backbone of modern Drupal applications, streamlining service management and boosting performance.

items.summary: Back Together Again: Lessons from Reviving a Community Post-COVID
items.description: When the world shut down, so did our meetups, camps, and energy. But coming back wasn’t just about restarting, it was about rebuilding something better. This is the real story of the Drupal India community revival.

items.summary: Building an AI-Powered DevOps Co-Pilot for Drupal: Automate Git, Drush & Deployments
items.description: What if your next patch, Drush command, or deployment script were drafted by an AI partner? Discover how to train and integrate an AI co-pilot into your Drupal DevOps pipeline.

items.summary: Drupal CMS AI and Google Gemini (Sponsored by Pantheon)
items.description: This session demonstrates what can be achieved with the combination of Drupal CMS’s AI capabilities, including the new Tools API, and the most advanced models available from Google Cloud Platform.

items.summary: Drupal Editor Showcase + Q&A
items.description: Drupal Canvas, Gutenberg, Blökkli - how do the different editors available for Drupal CMS compare? Join this showcase + Q&A session to find out.

items.summary: DrupalCamp England
items.description: After the successful gathering of our Drupal friends in Cambridge for the first interaction of DrupalCamp England in sprint 2025, we will be back for 2026!

items.summary: From starters to solid Drupal team members: strategies for finding and growing new talent in the Drupal community.
items.description: It’s no secret that finding, growing and retaining Drupal talent can be challenging.

items.summary: How to build a site template
items.description: How do you start Drupal sites the right way? Site templates! Learn everything you need to know to build awesome templates and share them with the world.

items.summary: Member Platform Initiative Discussion
items.description: Member Platform is a community initiative to build a Drupal based solution for membership organisations such as clubs, associations and non-profits (and let's not forget local Drupal associations).

items.summary: Leaving Work at Work – How to successfully finish your working day without thinking about work in your free time
items.description: Push your last commit, then push away from the desk. Clear your cache, recharge your brain. Great code starts with a well-rested mind. Prerequisite None

items.summary: Lunch Break, Wednesday
Women in Drupal Networking Event (Sponsored by JAKALA)
items.description: Join us for our Women in Drupal Networking Event!

items.summary: AI Bots & Drupal: Protecting Your Sites, Powering Your Content Speaker (Sponsored by Acquia Ltd)
items.description: This talk explores how AI bots are transforming the web and what that means for Drupal-powered enterprise sites.

items.summary: Protecting the Open Web: How Drupal and CrowdSec Team Up for Community Driven Security (Sponsored by CrowdSec)
items.description: Web security is a shared responsibility, and staying ahead of threats means acting proactively. In this talk, we will explore how the Drupal Association and CrowdSec have joined forces to bring community-powered protection directly into the CMS layer.

items.summary: The Web in 2035: A Keynote Panel on the Future of the Web
items.description: - What will the web look like in 10 years? Will it be open, fair, and sustainable - or fragmented, automated, and opaque?

items.summary: “We Need to Talk: Burnout, Belonging, and Being Human in Drupal”
items.description: This workshop can be a follow-up to other session(s) about mental health, which reflects on the emotional toll of todays society in the sense of burnouts and depressions. In this session, we’ll bring those ideas into action—through conversation, connection, and co-creation.

items.summary: £16 million of public funding for Open Standards, Open Source collaboration and Drupal
items.description: Since 2018 the Local Digital Fund has provided over £16 million of funding and support to 61 council-led projects in the UK, including LocalGov Drupal, Open Referral, Local Gov IMS and Open Digital Planning. What can we learn from this and how can we replicate the successes?

items.summary: AI vs. Human Creativity: How to Strike the Right Balance in Drupal
items.description: AI is transforming the way we build and manage digital experiences, but where does human creativity fit in? In this session, we’ll explore how to leverage AI as a tool to enhance—not replace—human creativity in Drupal.

items.summary: Contrib care program: Building a sustainable future for Drupal's essential modules
items.description: The Drupal ecosystem's strength lies in its rich collection of contributed modules, but this strength has become a vulnerability.

items.summary: Debugging techniques: the good, the bad, and the ugly
items.description: Debugging is an essential skill for software development. Attend this workshop to learn how to debug and solve pesky coding issues. Prerequisite Understanding of PHP and basic Drupal development skills are expected to take full advantage of this training.

items.summary: Drupal Canvas APIs: How to integrate and extend Canvas
items.description: Drupal Canvas will revolutionize how you will empower your editors and marketers to build sites with Drupal. But that might not be enough for your audience, and you will want to put guiderails, extend or improve the Experience of your Builders.

items.summary: Field Widget Actions: Bring Functionality Closer to Content Editors (Sponsored by 1xINTERNET)
items.description: Wouldn't it be great if your website could assist you while you're creating content, not just in the CKEditor, but also when you're tagging content, entering a title, or uploading an image?

items.summary: First-Time Contributor Workshop - Wednesday
From CMS to Platform: How to Build Future-Proof Digital Ecosystems with Drupal
items.description: Don't just build a website — build a platform. Learn how to design Drupal solutions that scale with your business, evolve with technology, and stay ahead of changing needs.

items.summary: Help! My search page is getting hammered by an AI bot!
items.description: AI bots are bringing websites down with never ending search queries. What can you as a site builder do about that? Which modules can help? And how do you get a better search experience at the same time.

items.summary: Oh no! undo! Revert recipes and AI configuration changes
items.description: You applied a recipe to your site? Or did you let an AI change some configuration on your site? It didn't do what you wanted? You want to go back! In this session you get to see how.

items.summary: Using DDEV on Windows
items.description: DDEV works great on Windows. WSL2 is the best way. We'll show how to install and get started. Bring your computer, it's easy!

items.summary: Coffee Break 2, Wednesday
Figma to Drupal Canvas — In Minutes, Not Months (Sponsored by Consumer mesh)
items.description: Join a leading enterprise and Consumer Mesh to explore real-world Drupal transformations: from Figma designs to fully functional sites, featuring stats, insights, and a live Figma-to-Canvas demo.

items.summary: Beyond Borders: Building Strong Open Source Communities from Local Roots to Global Collaboration
items.description: Imagine a community where every voice is heard, every contribution is valued, and the power of collaboration drives innovation. Now, picture that community stretching across organizations, regions, and even entire continents. Sounds like a dream?

items.summary: BYOPB: Understanding Project Browser's Architecture and Building Your Own Plugins
items.description: Unleash the power of Project Browser: discover how its pluggable architecture enhances project discovery, then learn how to develop your own Source Plugins to tap into any data source—from Google Sheets to self-hosted Git servers.

items.summary: Drupal.org Engineering Panel
items.description: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our work to support the community.

items.summary: Exploring Semantic Search and AI Agent Integration with Typesense in Drupal
items.description: Search is evolving fast, and so is Drupal. With the growing need for semantic search, vector databases, and AI-driven features, the Drupal community is experimenting with new ways to make content discovery smarter.

items.summary: How to Land an EPIC Contribution in Drupal (Without Losing Your Mind)
items.description: Behind every great Drupal feature is a stubborn person (not necessarily a developer) who refused to let it stay broken. Want to be that person? We’ll show you how.

items.summary: Institutional Intelligence: How Yale Uses Drupal to Transform Bureaucratic Complexity into Conversational Simplicity
items.description: Journey inside Yale's digital transformation as we reveal how our Drupal-based YaleSites platform has become the foundation for Beacon—an AI system that transforms bureaucratic complexity into intuitive conversations.

items.summary: LocalGov Drupal - where next?
items.description: An open discussion about the LocalGov Drupal distribution, the LocalGov Microsites distribution, the community, the features, the funding, the opportunities... ... and where to take it next!

items.summary: Scaling Drupal as a Headless Enterprise API: A High-Performance Case Study
items.description: This session presents a real-world case study of a large-scale enterprise application built on Drupal as a headless API, designed to handle a high volume of data—both fresh and migrated from Drupal 7. We’ll dive into key challenges and solutions, including:

items.summary: Status and Goals of the Drupal Open University Initiative
items.description: Just one year after the initial BoF in Barcelona, the Drupal Open University has grown beyond the wildest imagination of the attendees. Over 100 people contribute to the initiative with suggestions and contributions.

items.summary: The AI Agent Swarm has come to Drupal Canvas!
items.description: The Agent Swarm has come to Drupal Canvas and nothing in Drupal will ever be the same again! Since Barcelona, we have seen glimpses of how AI could help migrate designs and certain content into Drupal, but now with the release of Drupal Canvas, it’s finally here!

items.summary: AIO automation testing framework - we've built it, so you don't have to!
items.description: Supercharge your QA automation with Cuppet - the all-in-one Node.js framework designed to streamline functional, performance, SEO, and accessibility testing - for free!

items.summary: Carbon Impact Evaluator: Measuring and Reducing Drupal's Digital Carbon Footprint
items.description: In a world that values sustainability, the "Carbon Impact Evaluator" is essential for web developers and technology companies to reduce the environmental footprint of their websites.

items.summary: Drupal Dev Days 2026 - Brainstorming micro-symposium
items.description: Join us in a discussion about the upcoming Drupal Developer Days 2026 that will be held in Athens, Greece from 22nd to 25th of April 2026 (more details in https://devdays2026.drupal.org.gr/).

items.summary: Drush 14 is slimmed and ready for adoption by Drupal core
items.description: Drush 14 is a rewrite of all of its commands. We have trimmed a lot of special sauce in favor of doing things the native Symfony Console way. Our end game is for Drush to go away in favor of our commands in Drupal Core or CMS.

items.summary: From Recipe Platform to Consumer Hub - The Maggi.de Drupal Evolution
items.description: Explore Maggi.de's transformation from a recipe platform to a thriving consumer hub. Discover how Drupal's power enabled Maggi to deliver personalized, innovative services, driving user engagement and setting new standards in the food industry.

items.summary: Hooks, from magic names to php language feature
items.description: In bygone versions you could extend Drupal by creating a special module file and write php functions with magic names like mymodule_block_info or mymodule_form_alter. In Drupal 11 almost all of that code lives in particular places and has php attributes but it doesn't have to be frightening.

items.summary: NEDA community meeting for European Local Associations, DrupalCamps and Drupal User Groups
items.description: This session is intended for leaders and organizers from local European Drupal associations, DrupalCamps, other community initiatives, as well as representatives from the Drupal Association (DA).

items.summary: Smart Routing Between Legacy and Modern Drupal Sites with Cloudflare
items.description: Still running Drupal 7, rebuilding in Drupal 10/11, and stuck with some on-prem along the way? What if your traffic could pick the right path automatically, all while using one domain? Join us for a routing adventure through legacy, modern, and everything between!

items.summary: AI Content Migrate for Drupal: Automating Large-Scale Content Migrations with AI
items.description: Speed up website migrations and cut costs: discover how AI can automate content analysis, simplify workflows, and help you migrate your old Drupal website into a new one faster and smarter.

items.summary: From Chaos to Clarity: Planning for success with a Drupal Content Architecture
items.description: Great sites start with great plans: how the process of creating a content architecture can transform your next Drupal build — better, faster, stronger, and ready to grow.

items.summary: From Zero to Community: Building and Scaling Your Local Drupal Group
items.description: Ever wondered how to build a strong Drupal community in your city? Learn the actionable steps to start, grow, and sustain a local Drupal community from scratch — lessons learned while building Drupal Kerala community.

items.summary: Profiling your Drupal app with New Relic & DDEV: A practical guide
items.description: Is your Drupal app still facing performance issues despite all the efforts? Discover how to profile your app locally with New Relic and DDEV to uncover deeper code issues affecting the performance.

items.summary: Redefining Leadership: How Adversity can cultivate Strength, Empathy, and Leadership Skills
items.description: Leadership is often seen through the lens of strength, vision, and tireless energy. We picture the unshakable leader; the one who always shows up, ready to carry the weight of the world. But what happens when life delivers a blow so powerful, it shakes even the strongest among us?

items.summary: Morning Coffee, Thursday
General Contribution Day 3
Building AI Agents, Tools, and Assistants in Drupal: A Hands-On Workshop
items.description: In this hands-on workshop, you’ll learn how to build AI Tools, Agents, and Assistants inside Drupal CMS using the ai_agents module. We’ll code together, step-by-step, covering best practices for designing agents and evaluating agent performance.

items.summary: DDEV New Features
items.description: Let's look at the new features added to DDEV this year, including XHGui for studying performance, the Windows installer, and many more. Bring your questions and your feature requests!

items.summary: Drupal Business Survey 2025
items.description: The Drupal Business Survey investigates trends in the Drupal market based on data provided by digital service providers from all over the world.

items.summary: Extend your Drupal Watchdog!
items.description: Do you still use Database Logging in Drupal? Or already switched to the syslog, files, cloud? In both cases - you're cooking the logs wrong! And I'll explain why! Prerequisite A couple of looks at Drupal logs and an attempt to find something useful there.

items.summary: Imposter Syndrome's bigger, badder sibling: Shame
items.description: So much of our lives is spent working. We talk about work/life balance. And sometimes we talk about harder things, like Imposter Syndrome, or burnout, or "mental health". But we rarely talk about mental illness or about how our emotions affect our lives and our work.

items.summary: Migrating anything and everything to Drupal Canvas
items.description: You've heard about Drupal Canvas and it looks great! But... you are already using Layout Builder or Paragraphs or [insert some Drupal tech here] and you aren't sure how you can go from where you are to leveraging the goodness of Canvas. No worries... we've got you covered!

items.summary: No more steep learning curve! How UX research is making a more user-centric Drupal CMS (and how you can help
items.description: A UX mindset is our superpower in making Drupal CMS the gold standard for non-technical audiences. As Drupal CMS UX Research Lead, I’ll share how UX research is driving a product that’s changing the narrative about Drupal.

items.summary: PRESERVING LEGACY, EMPOWERING FUTURE: HOW DRUPAL POWERS AMI’S GLOBAL DIGITAL NETWORK
items.description: How do you manage a diverse ecosystem of digital experiences while ensuring scalability, accessibility and streamlined content sharing? The Association Montessori Internationale (AMI) has found its answer in Drupal.

items.summary: Shaking Up the Travel Industry with Drupal: Why Drupal Was the Catalyst for Riviera Travel’s Sector-Leading Transformation.
items.description: Discover how Drupal unlocked Riviera Travel’s ability to deliver seamless customer journeys, unify complex data relationships, and accelerate ahead of industry expectations - all in a sector not often seen as a Drupal use case.

items.summary: Leading Through the Storm: My Journey with Mental Health to the C-Suite
items.description: The unspoken burden of mental health can silently shape our lives and careers. For decades, I navigated leadership roles unaware of my own challenges. Discover my journey to the C-suite and learn how bringing awareness and openness can transform individuals and your workplace culture.

items.summary: Coffee Break 1, Thursday
Revolutionising UK Government Research with Drupal: transforming a paper-based medical research agency into a digital-first organisation (Sponsored by Reading Room)
items.description: Discover how Drupal empowered the Clinical Practice Research Datalink (CPRD) to transition from a paper-based system into a digital-first platform, streamlining research applications, enhancing user experience, and advancing public health research across the UK.

items.summary: Accessible by Default: How Drupal 11 Redefines Inclusive Design
items.description: Accessibility is no longer an afterthought — Drupal 11 builds it into every site by default. Discover how Single Directory Components, Recipes, and real-time tools empower you to create inclusive, scalable experiences effortlessly.

items.summary: Better Debugging with Xdebug
items.description: In this talk I explain how to use Xdebug to get more productive writing PHP code, focussing on the improvements in Xdebug 3.3 and 3.4, and the upcoming 3.5, to make the debugging experience better and easier to set up.

items.summary: Beyond 99 Red Balloons: a guide to alternative text and accessible images
items.description: Ready to make your content sing? Join us for a deep dive into inclusive content creation, alt text best practices, and how 80s imagery can help bring it all to life. Let’s ensure every reader feels the beat! Prerequisite None Outline

items.summary: Decision-Making at Scale: Behind the Drupal Marketplace Process
items.description: Making big decisions in open source isn't magic—it's intentional. Learn how the Drupal community is co-creating the future of our ecosystem through transparency, collaboration, and shared leadership.

items.summary: Drupal Canvas Accessibility
items.description: Let's gather those interested in improving the accessibility of Drupal Canvas and formulate plans to make it better.

items.summary: Drupal Without Limits: Expanding Drupal's reach into IoT, Commerce, and application ecosystems.
items.description: As technology rapidly advances, Drupal’s adaptability positions it at the forefront of innovation, powering IoT platforms, cutting-edge applications, and sophisticated commerce solutions.

items.summary: Europa Web Platform: Where today meets tomorrow (Sponsored by Drupal Community of Practice (CoP) at the European Commission )
items.description: Join us to discover how the EuropaIT Team of the European Commission is shaping the future of digital platforms through open source, innovation, and collaboration.

items.summary: Local Drupal Regions & Associations Round Table
Securing Drupal Content with Client-Side Encryption: A Zero Trust Approach
items.description: Imagine everyone was a royal. If that was the case, privacy would not be enough. We would all have top notch secrecy.

items.summary: The Future of Login: How Drupal Can Champion User Choice, Privacy, and Simplicity
items.description: Login today often limits users to a few big tech providers -and compromises their privacy. Let's explore how Drupal can lead a new movement: one where users choose how they log in, safely and freely.

items.summary: Building web experiences for the UK's biggest free TV platforms
items.description: Everyone TV is a not-for-profit joint venture owned by the BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and 5 ,who run the UK's biggest free TV platforms: Freely, Freeview and Freesat. Learn how Drupal is at the heart of Everyone TV's web experiences.

items.summary: How to sell Drupal site templates
items.description: After Dries Buytaert introduced site templates at DrupalCon Atlanta, Centarro released a completely retooled Commerce Kickstart to use the concept as a sales strategy. We set a goal to make our first template sales before DrupalCon Vienna, and we found success within weeks.

items.summary: Building AI Agents without coding
items.description: This talk will showcase how the Agents can be used everywhere in Drupal for helping with everything from menial small tasks to supercharging you when building your website from the ground up with external tools via MCP. All without having write a single line of code.

items.summary: Digital Sovereignty by Design
items.description: Digital sovereignty is a topic on the rise, but true digital sovereignty cannot happen without the freedoms of open source software. Let's look at what digital sovereignty really is and how you add true "digital sovereignty by design" to your sales pitch.

items.summary: Drupal, the first design-system native CMS
items.description: Drupal Core is accelerating its adoption of design-system related API started in 2023 with SDC, revolutionizing its theming workflow. Inspired by UI Suite proposals, our beloved Drupal is becoming the first design-system native CMS.

items.summary: Get started with your Local Association Website using the shared La_eu project
items.description: Are you part of a local Drupal association or community group looking to launch or modernize your website? The La_eu shared codebase offers an open, flexible solution built and maintained collaboratively by European Drupal associations.

items.summary: Improving and contributing Drupal Documentation. 2025 edition
items.description: Lately we've organised a series of help contribution sprints focused on reviewing, updating, and enhancing Drupal documentation based on feedback from the Slack documentation channel.

items.summary: Keeping the Commons Open: Ensuring Drupal's Sustainability
items.description: What happens when a digital commons reaches its breaking point? As Drupal grows, so does the risk to our shared ecosystem.

items.summary: Multilingual Drupal Canvas
items.description: Drupal Canvas does not support multilingual use cases at the start. Let's meet to discuss potential use cases, UI elements, workflows, and so on. Organizations and individuals interested to help implement it are especially welcome, but also don't be shy to come if you can't contribute (yet).

items.summary: Privacy by Design in Drupal (Sponsored by Hinto Group)
items.description: To enhance user experience, brand trust and awareness in modern web architecture, designing with privacy in mind has become a crucial aspect of digital design.

items.summary: Why and how to write your own compiler?
items.description: Writing a compiler is almost always perceived as a task not intended for high-level languages such as PHP. If you believe so, allow me to convince you otherwise. Through real-world examples, knowledge of how compilers work, and their benefits, will be demonstrated.

items.summary: Lunch Break, Thursday
Drupal Hosting in the Age of AI (Sponsored by Acolono)
items.description: We enable organizations to run secure, resilient, and high-performance Drupal hosting platforms. We’ll introduce Buzztion, an all-in-one edge solution that unifies caching, security, and metrics in a single lightweight package. Learn how to:

items.summary: Drupal CMS Spotlights
items.description: Meet key Drupal CMS leaders to get insights on how the platform is being built and learn about future plans. Topics include Drupal Canvas, AI, design systems and more. BÁLINT KLÉRI ELLIOTT MOWER GÁBOR HOJTSY GIORGI JIBLADZE KRISTEN POL PIERRE DUREAU

items.summary: AI in EAA?
items.description: What is the European Accessibility Act and how can AI help you in your journey to become compliant Prerequisite Nothing really. I'll explain all of the EAA and accessibility concepts from ground zero.

items.summary: Beyond Searching - Leveraging the Search API infrastructure for different features
items.description: Replacing dblog by Solr Log, implementing Cross Language Information Retrieval (CLIR) with TMGMT, integrating Default Content Deploy with Search API. Prerequisite Basic Drupal site-building knowledge, no coding skills required

items.summary: Drupal AI - strategy and application
items.description: Drupal has become the most advanced open-source AI orchestration platform in the world. With the Drupal AI module and its growing ecosystem, it’s now possible to automate content workflows, power advanced search, create digital assistants, and personalise user experiences.

items.summary: From Chaos to Consistency: Streamlining Data Analytics in Drupal Multisite Projects
items.description: In this case study session, discover how a single solution can power analytics for 50+ Drupal websites. Learn how a reusable Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4 framework delivers consistent web-tracking and rapid insights at scale.

items.summary: Git Bisect for Fun and Profit
items.description: `git bisect` is an amazing tool and surprisingly easy to use. You don't have to remember much at all to use it. You can find out so very easily where a bug was added in your commit history. We'll use a real hands-on example.

items.summary: How to concentrate in a meeting
items.description: We've all been there... at a meeting where your mind just starts wandering off... Even in meetings that aren't actually boring. What are our tricks to stay focused? Doodling? Walking around during a zoom call? Knitting? Drawing notes?

items.summary: Issue Triage
Lean digital service design with Drupal: Lessons from London's Southwark Council
items.description: How do you digitally transform hundreds of public services without blowing the budget? Discover how Southwark Council cracked the code—saving resources for bigger challenges, accelerating delivery, and seamlessly integrating legacy systems.

items.summary: Masterclass: Scaling sales and marketing success for Drupal agencies
items.description: Many Drupal agencies excel at technical delivery. A recent survey ran by the Drupal Executives Network shows Drupal agencies would benefit from support with structured sales and marketing.

items.summary: Test all the things!
items.description: All other checks and actions apart from Unit and Browsing testing that can be added to a project to quality assurance: accessibility, performance, API testing, static analysis and other tests.

items.summary: Thinking Async
items.description: With the introduction of the Revolt event loop in Drupal Core, we're getting ready for Drupal to tackle an entirely new class of problems: asynchronous applications. In this session you'll find out how to think asynchronously and leverage Revolt in your applications.

items.summary: From the Starting Line to the Finish: A Digital Sprint Toward Scalable Transformation
items.description: The Story Begins In the fast-paced world of endurance sports, success isn’t just measured at the finish line—it’s about delivering seamless, memorable experiences from the moment an athlete signs up to when they cross the tape.

items.summary: Coffee Break 2, Thursday
Declarative Shadow DOM and the future of Drupal Theming
items.description: For a frontend developer, Drupal is old-school. For the past decade, developers have been experimenting with JavaScript-based frontend frameworks that completely replace Drupal's frontend. But what if we could inject a fully modern JavaScript API into Drupal's rendering system?

items.summary: From Reactive to Proactive: Automated Performance Testing Success Stories
items.description: Gander, Drupal's performance testing framework introduced in Drupal core 10.3 and implemented for Drupal CMS 1.0, is markedly improving Drupal’s performance.

items.summary: Green UX in the Age of AI: Digital Products for a Sustainable Future
items.description: Can your next digital project help save the planet? Discover how Green UX and responsible AI can reduce your digital footprint—without sacrificing user experience or business goals.

items.summary: Make your own Drupal CMS: from distribution/profile to recipe based installation
items.description: For every new project, there are certain tasks we have to complete over and over again. We install the same modules, because we are used to having them, we configure features in a consistent way, implementing best practices or the optimal approaches that we know work well.

items.summary: Onboarding and Growing New Drupal Developers: Our 45-Day Internship Blueprint
items.description: Finding skilled Drupal talent is hard — so why not build it yourself? This session shares the structured 45-day plan we used to successfully onboard and mentor a batch of interns into confident junior Drupal developers, blending hands-on projects with best practice learning.

items.summary: Supercharging Drupal with Real-Time Notifications Using WebSockets and Redis
items.description: Imagine your Drupal site delivering live updates, pushing real-time notifications, or powering a chat system without the need to refresh the page. And more importantly, without depending on Firebase, Pusher, or any commercial messaging service.

items.summary: The Future of Drupal Theming: AI, Drupal Canvas, and Beyond
items.description: Drupal’s theming layer has grown by leaps and bounds over the past several years. We’ve seen new Twig filters, theme generation tools, and the introduction of Single Directory Components.

items.summary: The Future of the Search API Typesense Module
items.description: Join us to explore the latest improvements and shape the future of the **Search API Typesense** module—the bridge between Drupal's Search API and the powerful Typesense search engine.

items.summary: Drupal CMS: What's in it for me?
items.description: Are you struggling to navigate Drupal CMS’s seismic shift? With no-code and AI tools reshaping everything—including client and management expectations—adaptation is key, or we risk going the way of the dinosaurs...

items.summary: Fueling the Future: How Drupal and Open Source Propel the Airline Industry Towards Carbon Neutrality
items.description: This session highlights a success story in how Drupal powers a data-driven solution for tracking sustainable fuel usage, which in turn supports the EU's ambitious goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2050 (55% reduction by 2030, compared to 1990 levels).

items.summary: Drupal Fitness Award (Sponsored by DrupalFit)
items.description: We plan to access the visitors sites during the event and award 3 best sites at the end of the event.

items.summary: A Tour of Drupal CMS Recipes: Best Practices for Modern Site Building
items.description: Discover what’s inside Drupal CMS Recipes with a fast-paced, insightful tour. See what’s being delivered, uncover new best practices, and explore how these pre-configured solutions can enhance your site-building workflow.

items.summary: Beyond the Build: Architecting Resilient Client Journeys with Drupal
items.description: It’s not just about building Drupal sites — it’s about building client journeys that evolve, convert, and last. This session explores how to architect experiences that scale with real-world clients from the education, event and insurance sector.

items.summary: ECA, AI and MCP: Connecting Context, Intelligence and Action
items.description: Discover how integrating AI with Drupal's ECA module can revolutionize your workflows. Learn to use AI for chat, text-to-speech, and more, and meet an AI agent that simplifies model management.

items.summary: It’s OK to NOT be OK! Establishing support frameworks in community teams
items.description: Balancing volunteering and community responsibilities with personal and professional obligations can be stressful; it's difficult to know who to turn to for support.

items.summary: Mastering the release flow - over 5 years of continuous improvement
items.description: Transform your risky production deployments into a smooth, reliable process. Learn from 5+ years of continuously improving the release flow for a platform handling 40+ sites.

items.summary: Software Update Security for Humans: A gentle introduction
items.description: The Update Framework (TUF) is a standard being adopted across the open web, as a collaboration between open source projects to improve supply chain security. Learn about how this collaboration came to be, what TUF is, and why it was chosen to support Drupal's Automatic Updates initiative.

items.summary: Top 10 Composer Problems in Drupal Projects: Solutions from Real-World Sites
items.description: Are Composer issues causing headaches in your Drupal projects? Discover the top 10 real-world Composer mistakes and how to fix them, so you can build cleaner, safer, and more maintainable Drupal sites.

items.summary: Designing with Purpose: How to Integrate Your Features into Drupal Canvas
items.description: Learn how Drupal Canvas was designed to grow with you — and how you can extend it without compromising usability, consistency, or performance.

items.summary: Kubernetes-Powered Drupal: Architecting Scalable, Resilient Web Experiences
items.description: In an era where website performance directly impacts business outcomes, Kubernetes empowers Drupal developers to build infinitely scalable, self-healing architectures.

items.summary: SEO in the Age of AI Search: What’s Really Changing (and What’s Not)
items.description: Captivating Introduction

items.summary: Taming the Beast: Navigating Scope Creep for Project Success
items.description: Scope creep is one of the most silent but deadly threats to any software project — derailing timelines, blowing budgets, and clouding project goals.

items.summary: The Art of Doing Less: Let the Community Code Your Module
items.description: The open web benefits from collaboration of many people on projects, but how can you encourage people to contribute to yours?

items.summary: Wrap Up Ceremony Auditorium - DrupalCon 2026 city Announcement
Trivia Night
Drupal in a Day Seminar
items.description: Schedule 9:00 Opening 9:15 Drupal in a Day - Part 1 12:30 Lunch break 14:00 Drupal in a Day - Part 2 15:30 Photo and coffee break 16:30 Closing notes, and presentation of certifications

items.summary: First-Time Contributor Workshop - Friday
General Contribution Day 4
Mentored Contribution
Coffe Break 1, Friday
Lunch Break, Friday
Coffe Break 2, Friday
